rules.voice: first person as Andrew, not as Echo
rules.style: specific and technical, real code, real numbers, real failures, never generic

voice_notes: Andrew lives in Mena Arkansas. Trades background. No CS degree. Three kids under 5. Builds because he has to. Honest about failures. Echo is real infrastructure not a demo. Write like talking to another builder.

queue.title: How I built a two-way phone bridge for my AI using ntfy.sh
queue.angle: Echo listens for messages from my phone and replies autonomously. Real code, boot timing bugs, the self-fix.

queue.title: The regret index: how I gave my AI a conscience
queue.angle: What it is, why I built it, how it catches its own failure patterns.

queue.title: My AI traded stocks while I was on vacation
queue.angle: What the trading brain did, what signals it looked for, why it found nothing, what that means.

queue.title: What it actually costs to run an autonomous AI agent for 30 days
queue.angle: Real numbers: electricity, hardware, Golem earnings zero, dev.to views, lessons.

queue.title: Why my Golem node never got a task
queue.angle: CGNAT, Starlink, the old AT&T IP it was broadcasting for weeks.

queue.title: I built a self-healing AI and here is what actually broke
queue.angle: 182 junk drafts overnight, RAM pressure, boot sequencing bugs, what Echo learned.

queue.title: How Echo publishes to dev.to without me
queue.angle: The full pipeline, what went wrong, how it got fixed.

queue.title: Building toward the shop: voice AI in a noisy environment
queue.angle: Why this matters, what is blocking it, the VAD problem, where it is headed.
